figures.caption: Figure 9: User prompts driving the GPT-4o-mini contamination generator.
Figure 10: Prompts for GPT-4o-mini to judge the score of each trajectory step.

Table 2: Step-level correctness prediction performance on GTA and ToolBench. Each evaluation point is a single (prefix, evaluation turn) pair labelled correct or incorrect, not a trajectory-level outcome. AUROC (higher is better) and ECE (lower is better) are reported for each method. All AUROC and ECE values are reported as mean over 3 random seeds (42, 43, 44).

Figure 7: Hyperparameter analysis on GTA. (a) Peak PAIR-momentum success as a function of the momentum scaling α at fixed β = 0, lr = 3×10−7. The sweet spot lies at α ∈ {5, 10, 20}, all comfortably above the strongest vanilla PAIR baseline (dashed line); α ≤ 2 provides too little momentum signal to recover the within-group variance needed for GRPO advantage estimation. (b) A small KL anchor β = 0.01 improves peak success by +0.018 over β = 0 at fixed α = 5. (c) A larger learning rate 1×10−6 reduces peak success by 0.016 relative to 3×10−7 at fixed α = 5, β = 0, and yields a more chaotic per-step trajectory. Reference horizontals: BASE (no RL training, dotted) and the strongest vanilla-PAIR run (dashed).
Figure 4: Transfer performance on HotpotQA. Each method is trained on GTA and evaluated on HotpotQA without further fine-tuning. PAIR achieves the best result among all 16 methods.
Table 7: GRPO training configuration. All 16 method comparisons share these settings; only the per-step reward function differs.

Table 6: Per-step cost of each reward source. Latencies are measured on a single A6000 GPU with Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct as the policy. Additional forward passes counts forward passes in addition to the policy’s generation pass, which all methods incur. PAIR reuses the activations already produced by the generation pass, so it adds no marginal forward pass.
Figure 6: Ablation over the four attention statistics in PAIR’s Stage 2.
Figure 8: System prompts driving the GPT-4o-mini contamination generator.
Table 3: Overall performance comparison across all methods on GTA and ToolBench. Values are reported as mean±std over 3 random seeds (42, 43, 44).
Figure 5: Correctness prediction performance of linear probing models regarding to different contamination distances.
